confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_ffeed7c4-cab6-4cb7-a286-b041eddbf48d read back as received

observations.detail: narada_cli_readiness resolves packages/layers/cli/dist/main.js from process.cwd(), so running the shim from a User Site, PC Site, or arbitrary directory can falsely report CLI not ready.

wsl_path_translation warns whenever execution surface is not wsl_assisted, even for native Windows execution where WSL translation is not required. Native Windows should likely be pass or not_applicable.
Tool availability is probed by calling every command with --version. This is brittle for wt, yasb, and yasbc; command resolution should be separated from semantic readiness/read-back probes.

--execute initializes the User Site before the PC Site. If User succeeds and PC fails, the paired bootstrap can leave a half-created state. A two-phase preflight-then-execute path or explicit partial-state repair evidence is needed.

With --execute, adapter_plan entries become dry_run:false while mutation_performed remains false and execute_required remains true. This makes adapter execution state ambiguous; adapter plans should be marked planned_only or split into owning-locus execution commands.

Docs show bootstrap-windows --execute and also say adapter mutation requires explicit --execute at the owning locus. The wording can be read as the same flag, but the implementation does not mutate adapters. Docs should name the follow-up owning-locus commands or explicitly mark adapter execution as planned-only.

payload.suggested_direction: Keep bootstrap-windows as the front door, but separate cwd-independent CLI readiness, native-vs-WSL execution-surface posture, two-phase paired Site execution, and planned-only adapter topology from actual owning-locus adapter mutation.
payload.summary: Review of narada sites bootstrap-windows found that the Windows 11 front door shape is coherent, but several implementation details can mislead operators or create partial state.
payload.title: Windows bootstrap front door review surfaced readiness and execution ambiguities
